I don't have Windows machine, right now, to test it. Maybe someone with Windows machine could help here.
According to this patch, I think it's using `clang`

> How can you easily select gcc vs clang?

The user can specify `--linker-path=gcc<or>clang` to switch between the compilers. Or one can also use the env variable, `export LFORTRAN_CC=gcc`.

--linker-path has higher priority to choose the compiler.
> do you need to provide full path to them?

I think it's not required, if it is available in the path. If it says `clang not found`, then user should make sure to give the correct path to the compiler, right?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2512185878/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526","id":2706618543,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6hU7iv","number":5526,"title":"Possible fixes for linker issues","user":{"login":"Thirumalai-Shaktivel","id":74826228,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0ODI2MjI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74826228?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","html_url":"https://github.com/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-30T04:39:11Z","updated_at":"2024-12-07T04:55:24Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We need to support the following options how to link:
* gcc
* clang
* msvc
* LLVM lld (linked with LFortran)

We need to make it configurable, allow the user to tell LFortran how he wants the linking to be done, and where the linker.

I recommend not doing `which`, but rather just calling `gcc` or `clang` from PATH. The user is responsible for putting it there. But we can then tell lfortran where it is via environment variables and/or command line options.

For the most smooth experience, let's get the `lld` working --- it's an extra dependency and it does make our lfortran binary quite large (last time I tried I think it was something like extra 50MB), but it would remove the dependency on an external linker, which would fix all these issues. For now let's make the `lld` optional, that way we can enable it at godbolt and other places where the extra 50MB is not a big deal.

_Originally posted by @certik in https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4764#issuecomment-2508050473_

{"action":"closed","number":5605,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5605","id":2221248771,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6EZZED","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5605","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5605.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5605.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5605","number":5605,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"fix: Add two new options `--linker` and `--linker-path`","user":{"login":"Thirumalai-Shaktivel","id":74826228,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0ODI2MjI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74826228?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","html_url":"https://github.com/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Now, there are two ways to specify the linker and their path:
- Using `--linker` and `--linker-path` options
- Using environment variable `LFORTRAN_LINKER` and `LFORTRAN_LINKER_PATH`

Usage:
```console
$ lfortran x.f90 --linker=gcc
$ lfortran x.f90 --linker-path=/usr/bin
$ lfortran x.f90 --linker=gcc --linker-path=/usr/bin
```

```console
$ export LFORTRAN_LINKER=gcc
$ export LFORTRAN_LINKER_PATH=/usr/bin/
$ lfortran x.f90
```

For the below Fortran program:
```f90

    implicit none
    integer :: x(1)
    data x / 1 /
end program main
```

On it's execution with LFortran, I get the below error:
```console

Platform: macOS ARM
LLVM: 11.1.0
Default target: arm64-apple-darwin23.6.0

source_filename = "LFortran"

.entry:
  %array_bound2 = alloca i32, align 4
  %array_bound = alloca i32, align 4
  %__1_t = alloca i32, align 4
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  %__1_t1 = alloca i32, align 4
  %x = alloca [1 x i32], align 4
  store i32 1, [1 x i32]* %x, align 4
  br i1 true, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %.entry
  store i32 1, i32* %array_bound, align 4
  br label %ifcont

else:                                             ; preds = %.entry
  br label %ifcont

ifcont:                                           ; preds = %else, %then
  %2 = load i32, i32* %array_bound, align 4
  %3 = sub i32 %2, 1
  store i32 %3, i32* %__1_t1, align 4
  br label %loop.head

loop.head:                                        ; preds = %loop.body, %ifcont
  %4 = load i32, i32* %__1_t1, align 4
  %5 = add i32 %4, 1
  br i1 true, label %then3, label %else4

then3:                                            ; preds = %loop.head
  store i32 1, i32* %array_bound2, align 4
  br label %ifcont5

else4:                                            ; preds = %loop.head
  br label %ifcont5

ifcont5:                                          ; preds = %else4, %then3
  %6 = load i32, i32* %array_bound2, align 4
  %7 = icmp sle i32 %5, %6
  br i1 %7, label %loop.body, label %loop.end

loop.body:                                        ; preds = %ifcont5
  %8 = load i32, i32* %__1_t1, align 4
  %9 = add i32 %8, 1
  store i32 %9, i32* %__1_t1, align 4
  %10 = load i32, i32* %__1_t1, align 4

  br label %loop.head

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %ifcont5
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %loop.end
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Stored value type does not match pointer operand type!

 [1 x i32]

 [1 x i32]

Description

For the below Fortran program:
```f90
program main
    implicit none
    integer :: x
    data x / 1 /
end program main

```

In current main the ASR generated is:
```clj
(TranslationUnit
    (SymbolTable
        1
        {
            main:
                (Program
                    (SymbolTable
                        2
                        {
                            x:
                                (Variable
                                    2
                                    x
                                    []
                                    Local
                                    (IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
                                    (IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
                                    Default
                                    (Integer 4)
                                    ()
                                    Source
                                    Public
                                    Required
                                    .false.
                                )
                        })
                    main
                    []
                    [(Assignment
                        (Var 2 x)
                        (IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
                        ()
                    )]
                )
        })
    []
)
```


while on this branch:
```clj
(TranslationUnit
    (SymbolTable
        1
        {
            main:
                (Program
                    (SymbolTable
                        2
                        {
                            x:
                                (Variable
                                    2
                                    x
                                    []
                                    Local
                                    (IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
                                    (IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
                                    Default
                                    (Integer 4)
                                    ()
                                    Source
                                    Public
                                    Required
                                    .false.
                                )
                        })
                    main
                    []
                    []
                )
        })
    []
)
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5693","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5693/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5693/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5693/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5693","id":2739636133,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6jS4el","number":5693,"title":"Fix a issue with Print in MLIR backend","user":{"login":"Thirumalai-Shaktivel","id":74826228,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0ODI2MjI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74826228?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","html_url":"https://github.com/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-14T07:11:32Z","updated_at":"2024-12-14T07:11:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"              There is a issue here in `builder->getStringAttr(fmt))`,
Consider an example:
```fortran
program expr2
integer :: x

x = (2+3)*5
print *, x
write(*,*) x

end program
```
For which, I get the output as,
```console
$ lfortran examples/expr2.f90 --backend=mlir  
 25
 25
 -28655536
```
Where, ` -28655536` shouldn't be printed

The reason is, the global string `printf_fmt_2` and `printf_fmt_1` value doesn't have a null char `\00` at the end.
```mlir
module @LFortran {
  llvm.mlir.global private @printf_fmt_2(" %d\0A")
  llvm.func @printf(!llvm.ptr<i8>, ...)
  llvm.mlir.global private @printf_fmt_1(" %d\0A")
  llvm.func @main(%arg0: !llvm.ptr<i8>, ...) -> i32 {
  // [...]
  }
}
```

I will submit a new PR to fix this.

_Originally posted by @Thirumalai-Shaktivel in https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5691#discussion_r1884870787_

lai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-14T07:11:32Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T06:13:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There is a issue here in `builder->getStringAttr(fmt))`,

Consider an example:
```fortran
program expr2
integer :: x

x = (2+3)*5
print *, x
write(*,*) x

end program
```
For which, I get the output as,
```console
$ lfortran examples/expr2.f90 --backend=mlir  
 25
 25
 -28655536
```
Where, ` -28655536` shouldn't be printed

The reason is, the global string `printf_fmt_2` and `printf_fmt_1` value doesn't have a null char `\00` at the end.
```mlir
module @LFortran {
  llvm.mlir.global private @printf_fmt_2(" %d\0A")
  llvm.func @printf(!llvm.ptr<i8>, ...)
  llvm.mlir.global private @printf_fmt_1(" %d\0A")
  llvm.func @main(%arg0: !llvm.ptr<i8>, ...) -> i32 {
  // [...]
  }
}
```

I will submit a new PR to fix this.

_Originally posted by @Thirumalai-Shaktivel in https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5691#discussion_r1884870787_

#5707

- When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program line","user":{"login":"kmr-srbh","id":151380951,"node_id":"U_kgDOCQXj1w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/151380951?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh","html_url":"https://github.com/kmr-srbh","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmr-srbh/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"fixes #5707

- When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program - When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program - When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program #5707

- When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program #5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

```
units
    : units script_unit  %dprec 9  { RESULT($2); }
    | script_unit        %dprec 10 { RESULT($1); }
    | sep
    ;

script_unit
    : module
    | submodule
    | block_data
    | program
    | subroutine
    | procedure
    | function
    | use_statement
    | implicit_statement
    | var_decl           %dprec 9
    | derived_type_decl
    | enum_decl
    | statement          %dprec 7
    | expr sep           %dprec 8
    | KW_END_PROGRAM sep { $$ = SYMBOL($1, @$); }
    ;
```
This lines are responsible for recognising all the nodes in the TranslationUnit. 
So for your case as it doesn't have the program keyword. It won't be parsed through
```
script_unit
    : module
    | submodule
    | block_data
    | program     <-----
    | subroutine
[...]
``` 
But instead, get parsed through:
```
    | enum_decl
    | statement          %dprec 7
    | expr sep           %dprec 8.   <-----
```
Here, as you can see expr has higher precedence than statement. So, it is parsed as expr -> FuncCallOrArray.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2543894490/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

diff --git a/src/lfortran/parser/parser.yy b/src/lfortran/parser/parser.yy
index cf952e6bc..5047d3a06 100644
--- a/src/lfortran/parser/parser.yy
+++ b/src/lfortran/parser/parser.yy

     | var_decl           %dprec 9
     | derived_type_decl
     | enum_decl

-    | expr sep           %dprec 8
+    | statement          %dprec 8
+    | expr sep           %dprec 7
     | KW_END_PROGRAM sep { $$ = SYMBOL($1, @$); }
     ;
```

Can you check if it works for all your cases and does it passes the tests?

In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm

source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

#5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()

And it seems the passed values to
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/blob/381124d216e562cae68d9a008e556925637d93d2/src/libasr/codegen/asr_to_llvm.cpp#L2802
is 
```
@drratio = global [2 x i32] [i32 0, i32 0]
```
But LLVM represents it as 
```
@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
```

Maybe you can do this. 
Check if all the elements are zero if so then set it to NULL (aka zeroinitializer => getNullValue(...)).

- When no `program` line is used, we handle the `allocate` statement as a general `AST::FuncCallOrArray_t` node, which is also a `AST::expr_t`.

- Previously, we handled `AST::expr_t` only for `AST::Name_t` and skipped this case, causing errors.

- In the current implementation, we create the required `AST::Allocate_t` node and add to the program #5690 
In this PR i have changed 
```fortran
integer, dimension(2) :: drratio=0 --------> integer,dimension(2) :: drratio
real, dimension(2) :: drratior=0.0 ---------> real, dimension(2) :: drratior
```
On ASR level 
Which prevents this llvm error 
```clojure
aditya-trivedi   lfortran    main ≡    lfortran integration_tests/array_init_with_scalar.f90 --show-llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@drratio = global [2 x i32] zeroinitializer
@drratior = global [2 x float] zeroinitializer

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)

declare void @_lpython_free_argv()
asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior

code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x i32]* @drratio
Global variable initializer type does not match global variable type!
[2 x float]* @drratior



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

```

- OpenMP pass creates a module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading`, 
   which will have all the items required for GPU offloading
- In the LLVM backend,
  - We first visit this module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading` and 
     call the MLIR backend to generate MLIR and translate that MLIR to LLVM IR.
  - Now, use this LLVM IR's module to generate rest of the source code

This also includes other fixes:
- [MLIR] Allow LLVMContext as argument to mlir_to_llvm
- [MLIR] Create a new ModuleOp in visit_Module, if it's null
- [MLIR] Visit_asr instead of details:
- OpenMP pass creates a module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading`, 
   which will have all the items required for GPU offloading
- In the LLVM backend,
  - We first visit this module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading` and 
     call the MLIR backend to generate MLIR and translate that MLIR to LLVM IR.
  - Now, use this LLVM IR's module to generate rest of the source code

This also includes other fixes:
- [MLIR] Allow LLVMContext as argument to mlir_to_llvm
- [MLIR] Create a new ModuleOp in visit_Module, if it's null
- [MLIR] Visit_asr instead of visit_TranslationUnit","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5880/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5880/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2566299202","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5880#issuecomment-2566299202","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5880","id":2566299202,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y9p5C","user":{"login":"Thirumalai-Shaktivel","id":74826228,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0ODI2MjI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74826228?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","html_url":"https://github.com/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-31T09:53:25Z","updated_at":"2024-12-31T09:53:25Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"For the example:
```
program do_concurrent_01
  integer :: i, n
  real :: x(12)
  n = 12

  x = 0.49

  do concurrent(i = 1:n)
    print *, x(i)
  end do
end program
```

I get the dump fortran as 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
module _lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading
implicit none

contains

subroutine _lcompilers_doconcurrent_replacer_func_real____2(i, n, x, __1x, __2x)
    integer(4), intent(in) :: __1x
    integer(4), intent(in) :: __2x
    integer(4), intent(inout) :: i
    integer(4), intent(inout) :: n
    real(4), dimension(__1x:(__1x)+(__2x)-1), intent(inout) :: x
    do concurrent ( i = 1: n )
        print *, x(i)
    end do
end subroutine _lcompilers_doconcurrent_replacer_func_real____2

end module _lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading

program do_concurrent_01
use _lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading, only: _lcompilers_doconcurrent_replacer_func_real____2
implicit none
integer(4) :: __libasr_index_0_
integer(4) :: i
integer(4) :: n
real(4), dimension(12) :: x
n = 12
__libasr_index_0_ = lbound(x, 1) - 1
do while (__libasr_index_0_ + 1 <= ubound(x, 1))
    __libasr_index_0_ = __libasr_index_0_ + 1
    x(__libasr_index_0_) = 4.90000000e-01
end do
call _lcompilers_doconcurrent_replacer_func_real____2(i, n, x, 1, 12)
end program do_concurrent_01
```

Here, the module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading` would be generated by MLIR backend and rest of the code by LLVM backend.

Output:
```console
$ lfortran integration_tests/do_concurrent_01.f90 --openmp --openmp-lib-dir=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib --mlir-gpu-offloading

details:
- OpenMP pass creates a module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading`, 
   which will have all the items required for GPU offloading
- In the LLVM backend,
  - We first visit this module `_lcompilers_mlir_gpu_offloading` and 
     call the MLIR backend to generate MLIR and translate that MLIR to LLVM IR.
  - Now, use this LLVM IR's module to generate rest of the source code

This also includes other fixes:

As we use PointerToCPtr, instead of a symbol from lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding module. So, the ExternalSymbol becomes unused.

See the generated fortran code, after applying the pass:
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: unique_symbols

program fortran_01
use iso_c_binding, only: c_ptr
implicit none
type(c_ptr) :: ptr
integer(4), pointer :: x
ptr = c_loc(x)
end program fortran_01
```
And it will fail with gfortran.

It's not a blocker as we don't apply passes for fortran backend. So, it's fine for frontend"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-21T15:56:52Z","updated_at":"2024-11-23T05:41:05Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```fortran
program main
    real :: A(4)
    call sub(A)
    
    contains
    subroutine sub(A)
    real, intent(in) :: A(:)
    real :: xbdi(size(A)), ssq(size(A))
    xbdi = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    ssq = [1, 1, 1, 1]
    where (xbdi == [1, 2, 3, 4]) ssq = precision(ssq - xbdi)
    print *, ssq
    end subroutine
    
end program
```
```console
$ lfortran abc.f90 --continue-compilation
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```

Now when I comment out the above code and do the following:
```fortran
! program main
!     real :: A(4)
!     call sub(A)
    
!     contains
!     subroutine sub(A)
!     real, intent(in) :: A(:)
!     real :: xbdi(size(A)), ssq(size(A))
!     xbdi = [1, 2, 3, 4]
!     ssq = [1, 1, 1, 1]
!     where (xbdi == [1, 2, 3, 4]) ssq = precision(ssq - xbdi)
!     print *, ssq
!     end subroutine
    
! end program

program main
    integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
    print *, abs("ok")
end
```
```console
$ lfortran abc.f90 --continue-compilation
semantic error: Type mismatch in binary operator, the types must be compatible
  --> abc.f90:20:31
   |
20 |     integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
   |                               ^   ^^^^ type mismatch (integer and string)


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```

Consider the example:
I have a file x.f90 in the home folder, now I run:
```console
$ lfortran x.f90 --continue-compilation
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```
and the generated files are `x.out` and `x.tmp.o`

Now, I modify the file with your change:
```console
$ lfortran x.f90 --continue-compilation
semantic error: Type mismatch in binary operator, the types must be compatible
 --> x.f90:2:31
  |
2 |     integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
  |                               ^   ^^^^ type mismatch (integer and string)


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```
The output generated here is from the `x.tmp.o` file which is getting linked by `link_executable` here: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/blob/ba17f7c0216c6b35481723d68b6dda8301828057/src/bin/lfortran.cpp#L2671

Deleting the `x.tmp.o` file, throws the following:
```console
$ lfortran x.f90 --continue-compilation
semantic error: Type mismatch in binary operator, the types must be compatible
 --> x.f90:2:31
  |
2 |     integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
  |                               ^   ^^^^ type mismatch (integer and string)


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at

program main
    real :: A(4)
    call sub(A)
    
    contains
    subroutine sub(A)
    real, intent(in) :: A(:)
    real :: xbdi(size(A)), ssq(size(A))
    xbdi = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    ssq = [1, 1, 1, 1]
    where (xbdi == [1, 2, 3, 4]) ssq = precision(ssq - xbdi)
    print *, ssq
    end subroutine
    
end program
```
```console
$ lfortran abc.f90 --continue-compilation
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```

Now when I comment out the above code and do the following:
```fortran
! program main

!     contains

!     where (xbdi == [1, 2, 3, 4]) ssq = precision(ssq - xbdi)
!     print *, ssq
!     end subroutine
    
! end program

program main
    integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
    print *, abs("ok")
end
```
```console
$ lfortran abc.f90 --continue-compilation

  --> abc.f90:20:31
   |
20 |     integer, parameter :: x = 1 + "ok"
   |                               ^   ^^^^ type mismatch (integer and string)


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
6.00000000e+00
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

### TODO:

program main
    implicit none
    integer, parameter :: RP = kind(0.0D0)
    integer, parameter :: IK = kind(0)
    real(RP) :: G(2, 2)
    integer(IK) :: knew
    real(RP):: zmat(1:1, 1:2) 
    ! knew = 1
    G = planerot([zmat(knew, 1), zmat(knew, 2)])
    contains
    
    function planerot(x) result(G)
        real(RP), intent(in) :: x(:)
        real(RP) :: c, s, r 
        real(RP) :: G(2, 2)
        c = x(1) / r
        G = reshape([c, -s, s, c], [2, 2])
    end function
end program
```

program main
    integer, parameter :: RP = kind(0.0D0)
    integer, parameter :: IK = kind(0)
    real(RP) :: G(2, 2)
    integer(IK) :: knew
    real(RP):: zmat(1:5) 
    G = planerot(zmat([1, 2]))
    contains
    
    function planerot(x) result(G)
        real(RP), intent(in) :: x(:)
        real(RP) :: c, s, r
        real(RP) :: G(2, 2)
        c = x(1) / r
        G = reshape([c, -s, s, c], [2, 2])
    end function
end program
```
```console
$ lfortran a.f90
semantic error: Substring end index at must be of type integer
 --> a.f90:7:23
  |
7 |     G = planerot(zmat([1, 2]))
  |                       ^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5526","id":2706618543,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6hU7iv","number":5526,"title":"Possible fixes for linker issues","user":{"login":"Thirumalai-Shaktivel","id":74826228,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc0ODI2MjI4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74826228?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","html_url":"https://github.com/Thirumalai-Shaktivel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Thirumalai-Shaktivel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-30T04:39:11Z","updated_at":"2024-11-30T04:39:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We need to support the following options how to link:
* gcc
* clang
* msvc
* LLVM lld (linked with LFortran)

We need to make it configurable, allow the user to tell LFortran how he wants the linking to be done, and where the linker.

I recommend not doing `which`, but rather just calling `gcc` or `clang` from PATH. The user is responsible for putting it there. But we can then tell lfortran where it is via environment variables and/or command line options.

For the most smooth experience, let's get the `lld` working --- it's an extra dependency and it does make our lfortran binary quite large (last time I tried I think it was something like extra 50MB), but it would remove the dependency on an external linker, which would fix all these issues. For now let's make the `lld` optional, that way we can enable it at godbolt and other places where the extra 50MB is not a big deal.

_Originally posted by @certik in https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4764#issuecomment-2508050473_
